content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 26, 1986


Mr. James L. Larson
Attorney at Law
P.O. Box 1369
Fort Bragg, CA  95437

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-86-261

Dear Mr. Larson:

     In accordance with our telephone conversation, enclosed is a
copy of Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702.1
which we discussed.

     During our conversation, you informed me that the maximum
amount that rates could change under the rate setting proposal
would be approximately $85 to $100 per year.  You can see that
under regulation 18702.1(4) (copy enclosed), officials would
not be required to disqualify themselves unless the decision
could increase or decrease expenses by $250 or more per year.
The Commission has interpreted this provision to mean that the
$250 effect would occur within a one-year period.

     Accordingly, the members of the Noyo Harbor Commission and
the Noyo Harbor District would not be disqualified from voting
on the rate change proposals.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to call
me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        John G. McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JM:JT:km
Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

James L. Larson
ATTORNEY AT LAW
311 N. McPHERSON ST.     P.O. BOX 1369
FORT BRAGG, CA 95437

(707) 964-6327

August 14, 1986


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Ladies and Gentlemen:

     I am writing on behalf of the Noyo Harbor Commission
and the Noyo Harbor District, a California Port District, es-
tablished under the Harbors and Navigation Code, § 6200, et seq.

     A question has arisen regarding whether various
commission members are disqualified from voting on rate setting
proposals and on the over-all budget of the District.

     Because our budget by law is to be completed by
September 1, 1986, an immediate response to this letter is
requested.

     One of the board members is the owner and operator
of a commercial fishing vessel, another member is an owner and
the wife of an operator of a commercial fishing vessel, and
a third member is shown on the title to a vessel as a co-owner,
but exercises no control over the vessel and has no beneficial
interest therein.

     Your advice is requested as to the following:

     1. Are these members each disqualified from voting
or participating in rate setting decisions fixing mooring rates
for vessels in the Noyo Mooring Basin, which is operated by the
District?  (A relatively small percentage of the voters and tax
payers in the District are vessel owners whose vessels are moored
in Noyo Mooring Basin.)

     2. May one of the disqualified members vote on set-
ting rates (if selected in a random fashion) pursuant to
Government Code §87101?

-2-


3.  May the three members vote on the adoption
of a budget if a rate increase or decrease is not a part of the
budget?

     Thank you for your anticipated assistance.  If
you need any further information or would like to discuss the
matter further with the undersigned, please do not hesitate
to telephone my office at your convenience.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        JAMES L. LARSON, Attorney for
                                        Noyo Harbor District


     P.S.: By action of the board at its meeting of
August 14, 1986, I was directed to make this inquiry and request
your written advice.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 19, 1986


James L. Larson
Attorney at Law
311 N. McPherson St.
P.O. Box 1369
Fort Bragg, CA  95437

Re:  86-261

Dear Mr. Larson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received on August 19, 1986 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

                                   Very truly yours,


                                   John G. McLean
                                   Counsel
                                   Legal Division

sections.question: 1. Are these members each disqualified from voting or participating in rate setting decisions fixing mooring rates for vessels in the Noyo Mooring Basin, which is operated by the District? (A relatively small percentage of the voters and tax payers in the District are vessel owners whose vessels are moored in Noyo Mooring Basin.) 2. May one of the disqualified members vote on setting rates (if selected in a random fashion) pursuant to Government Code §87101? 3. May the three members vote on the adoption of a budget if a rate increase or decrease is not a part of the budget?
sections.conclusion: Accordingly, the members of the Noyo Harbor Commission and the Noyo Harbor District would not be disqualified from voting on the rate change proposals.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the Noyo Harbor Commission and District who own or have interest in commercial fishing vessels are disqualified from voting on mooring rate setting and budget decisions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under California regulation 18702.1(a)(4), officials are not required to disqualify themselves unless a decision could increase or decrease expenses by $250 or more per year, and since the maximum rate change would be approximately $85-$100 per year, the members are not disqualified from voting on the rate changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter and FPPC response letter. Primary response is dated August 26, 1986 with clear conclusion section. Acknowledgment letter dated August 19, 1986 also included. Extraction focuses on the substantive advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Are these members each disqualified from voting or participating in rate setting decisions fixing mooring rates for vessels in the Noyo Mooring Basin, which is operated by the District? (A relatively small percentage of the voters and tax payers in the District are vessel owners whose vessels are moored in Noyo Mooring Basin.) 2. May one of the disqualified members vote on setting rates (if selected in a random fashion) pursuant to Government Code §87101? 3. May the three members vote on the adoption of a budget if a rate increase or decrease is not a part of the budget?

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, the members of the Noyo Harbor Commission and the Noyo Harbor District would not be disqualified from voting on the rate change proposals.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to inquiry from Noyo Harbor Commission regarding whether board members with interests in commercial fishing vessels are disqualified from voting on mooring rate and budget matters. The FPPC concludes that members are not disqualified since the proposed rate changes ($85-$100 per year) fall below the $250 annual expense threshold requiring disqualification.
